levels.hint: Usa GROUP BY para agrupar filas por una columna, y COUNT para contar.
Sintaxis: SELECT columna_grupo, COUNT(*) AS alias FROM tabla GROUP BY columna_grupo; Necesitas agrupar por departamento y contar empleados.
Ejecuta: SELECT departments.name AS dept, COUNT(employees.id) AS count FROM employees JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id GROUP BY departments.id;
Usa AVG() para calcular el promedio de una columna.
Sintaxis: SELECT columna_grupo, AVG(columna_valor) FROM tabla GROUP BY columna_grupo; Necesitas agrupar por departamento y promediar salary.
Ejecuta: SELECT departments.name AS dept, AVG(employees.salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id GROUP BY departments.id;
Usa MAX() y MIN() para obtener los valores extremos.
Sintaxis: SELECT MAX(columna) AS maximo, MIN(columna) AS minimo FROM tabla; No necesitas GROUP BY porque buscas el maximo y minimo de toda la tabla.
Ejecuta: SELECT MAX(salary) AS max_salary, MIN(salary) AS min_salary FROM employees;
Usa CREATE TABLE AS para crear una tabla a partir de una consulta SELECT.
Sintaxis: CREATE TABLE nombre AS SELECT ... ; Necesitas hacer un GROUP BY de departamentos con COUNT, AVG y MAX de salary.

Ejecuta: CREATE TABLE dept_summary AS SELECT departments.id AS dept_id, COUNT(employees.id) AS total_employees, AVG(employees.salary) AS avg_salary, MAX(employees.salary) AS max_salary FROM employees JOIN departments ON employees.dept_id = departments.id GROUP BY departments.id;
